news.summary: On May 15, 2025, the Association for Diagnostics & Laboratory Medicine (ADLM) formally known as AACC, issued a communication directed to Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS). The ADLM appealed to HHS to reintroduce the Clinical Laboratory Improvements Act (CLIA) in order to ensure quality standards for laboratory testing. The letter emphasized the importance of CLIA in regulating over 251,000 laboratories in the United States to guarantee accurate test results and safe patient care.
news.headline: ADLM urges HHS to reinstate crucial committee for lab tests safety. Letter sent to Secretary Kennedy.

news.summary: A recent report from AMLBot revealed that a lag in Tether's wallet blacklisting process allowed over $78 million in illicit funds to be moved before enforcement actions could be implemented. The blacklisting process is delayed due to Tether's multisignature contract setup on Ethereum and Tron, creating a window of opportunity for malicious actors. The delay between the submission of a blacklist candidate and the actual blacklisting can be exploited by fraudsters, with over $28.5 million withdrawn on Ethereum and $49.6 million on Tron during these windows. The average amount moved during the delay exceeded $365,000. Tether has promoted its ability to freeze assets for compliance reasons but questions are now raised about the effectiveness and speed of these enforcement actions.
news.headline: $78M in illicit USDT transfers bypass Tether's blacklisting due to delays in enforcement on Ethereum and Tron, says AMLBot.

news.summary: London-based algorithmic crypto trading firm, Wintermute, has expanded into the US by opening an office in New York due to improved regulatory conditions. The firm sees the US as friendlier towards digital assets and institutional adoption, leading to their quick establishment in the city. Wintermute aims to contribute to the future regulatory framework in the US and appointed Ron Hammond, with ten years of crypto policy experience, as the head of policy and advocacy. Since President Trump's inauguration, eight crypto firms have expanded into the US, expecting more innovation-friendly regulations. Wintermute aims to contribute to the US regulatory framework, meeting with the SEC Crypto Task Force and supporting key legislative efforts. The article also mentions the STABLE Act and the GENIUS Act related to stablecoin legislation, signaling ongoing discussions in the crypto industry.
news.headline: Wintermute, a London crypto firm, opens NY office due to US crypto rule improvements. CEO aims to shape regulations. Related: More firms expand in the US. CEO appoints Ron Hammond as head of policy, bringing 10 years of experience. Trump's presidency boosts expectations for crypto-friendly regulations. 8 large firms announce US expansion, including Binance.US, eToro, and Nexo. Wintermute meets with SEC Crypto Task Force to contribute to regulatory framework. Stablecoin legislation progresses. Regulatory clarity remains active in both chambers.

news.summary: A report from AMLBot revealed a delay in Tether's wallet blacklisting process, allowing over $78 million in illicit funds to be moved before enforcement actions kicked in. The delay is due to Tether's multisignature contract setup on Ethereum and Tron. The blacklisting procedure involves two multisignature transactions, with a significant time gap between them, giving illicit actors a window to move or launder funds. AMLBot data showed over $28.5 million was withdrawn on Ethereum and $49.6 million on Tron during these delays, totaling $78.1 million. Approximately 4.88% of wallets on Tron exploited this lag to withdraw an average of $291,970. Tether had previously collaborated to freeze $126 million in illicit funds, but the report questions the effectiveness and speed of these enforcement actions.
news.headline: Delay in Tether blacklisting let $78M in USDT move illicitly before enforcement, due to lag in process, show report.

news.summary: Today, U.S. stocks saw a decline, with the Dow Jones index dropping over 150 points. The Dow was down by 0.39% at 41,888.10, the NASDAQ fell 0.83% to 18,987.25, and the S&P 500 dropped 0.44% to 5,866.61. Utilities shares surged by 1.2%, while health care stocks fell by 1.4%. Walmart Inc. reported better-than-expected earnings for Q1, with sales growing to $165.60 billion and adjusted EPS at 61 cents. For FY26, Walmart reiterated an adjusted EPS outlook of $2.50-$2.60 and sales guidance of $694.70 billion-$701.50 billion. Additionally, Click Holdings Limited shares surged by 270%. More details on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dow drops 150 points, Walmart beats earnings expectations. Dow at 41,888.10, NASDAQ at 18,987.25, S&P 500 at 5,866.61.

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell announced that the central bank is considering changes to its policy strategy regarding inflation and employment. The Fed is reevaluating its approach as part of a major review to be completed by 2025. Powell mentioned that participants have suggested revising how the Fed discusses employment and average inflation targeting. The Fed had adopted a strategy in 2020 to allow inflation to slightly exceed 2% to boost job growth, but inflation skyrocketed to 7.2% in 2022, leading to a significant interest rate hike. Powell indicated that the old policy may not be suitable anymore due to changing economic conditions. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Fed considering changes to inflation and employment strategies after reviewing in 2025. Powell suggests revising old policies as inflation peaked at 7.2% in 2022, leading to aggressive interest rate hikes of 5.25% in 16 months. Old approach may no longer be suitable due to evolving economic conditions.

news.summary: A renowned gymnastics label is expanding its collection to include swimwear, staying true to its reputation for providing comfort, quality, and empowering designs.
